- ⚠️ Conflicts with: the recording location — consequence.net (2013) describes the album as recorded in "Cook's basement studio," while AllMusic names "Glasgow's Alucard Studio"; the June 2015 Pitchfork studio feature corroborates the "basement flat" description, stating it is "the same space where Chvrches have recorded nearly all of their music since forming in late 2011" — strongly suggesting "Alucard Studio" is AllMusic's name for the same basement flat rather than a separate commercial facility. [4, 1, 5]
- The Bones of What You Believe entered the Australian album chart on 2013-10-06, peaked at #13, and spent 5 weeks on the chart, corroborating its entry into the Australian Top 20. [2]
- An Eric Prydz remix of "Tether," credited as "Tether (Eric Prydz vs. Chvrches)," was released in Australia as a commercial single on 2015-02-16 on Prydz's own Pryda label — a standalone digital release separate from the Vertigo TBOWYTB catalogue, per the australian-charts.com singles discography. [2]
- The Bones of What You Believe debuted at number nine on the U.K. albums chart; the Hostess product page records the chart date as October 5, 2013 (10/5付). [3, 6]
- The album was certified Silver in the UK by the BPI on 2014-03-21, and certified Gold on 2014-12-19. [3,5,12,17]
- The Bones of What You Believe entered the Top 20 on the charts in Canada, Australia, and the United States, among other countries. [3]
- "The Mother We Share" charted in the U.S., Japan, Belgium, and the U.K., where it was a Top 40 hit. [3]
- The Bones of What You Believe debuted and peaked at #12 on the Billboard 200 and #5 on Billboard's Top Rock Albums chart; the Hostess product page additionally records a debut at #1 on the US Indie chart, with a chart date of October 12, 2013 (10/12付). [7, 6, 16]
- The Bones of What You Believe debuted on Billboard's Top Alternative Albums chart on 10/12/13, peaked at #3 on its debut date, and spent 13 weeks on the chart. [8, 7]
- As of 2013-03 (the Paste Magazine interview preceding CHVRCHES' first LA show), CHVRCHES already had "an album's worth of material" assembled and were in the mixing stage; Iain Cook stated they were "leaving the door open for any last minute songs that might come through that may be contenders for the album," and the band planned a fall release for the LP. [9]
- As of early 2013-03, the band had told a live audience that their full-length debut should not be expected until the Fall; they described hoping to sustain the excitement that had built around the band from autumn 2012 until then. [18]
- The Bones of What You Believe sold approximately 182,000 copies in the United States, per the June 2015 Pitchfork studio feature. [5]
- The Bones of What You Believe had sold over 500,000 copies worldwide; the BBC interview published 2015-09-26 described this as "approximately half a million" without region qualification. This is consistent with the US-specific figure of 182,000 reported by Pitchfork in mid-2015. [11,13]
- Lauren Mayberry described TBOWYTB as "probably one of the very few self-written, self-recorded, self-produced records to make it into the UK Top 10" — a distinction she noted while pointing to the three keyboards and a microphone used to record it. In a separate 2015 interview she elaborated: "our album was in the Top 10 in the U.K. for half a minute, which was amazing and insane, and it was probably the only record in the Top 10 that was recorded on a freestanding mic stand in the middle of a room in Glasgow." [5, 10]
- The core recording equipment for The Bones of What You Believe comprised just three keyboards and one microphone, preserved at the Glasgow studio at the time of the June 2015 Pitchfork interview. [5]
- The Bones of What You Believe debuted and peaked at #19 on the Billboard Canadian Albums chart (chart week 2013-10-12), spending 1 week on the chart. [14]
- The Bones of What You Believe was released in the UK on Virgin Records, credited as "Virgin; 2013" in the sub-heading of a Coke Machine Glow track essay published 2013-07-09 — consistent with BPI records for "The Mother We Share" listing "UNIVERSAL MUSIC VIRGIN" as the UK rights holder. [19]

Caption: The Bones of What You Believe Tour (2012–2014)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- The TBOWYTB tour comprised 365 gigs across two years. [1, 2]
- ⚠️ Conflicts with: the SXSW 2016 official CHVRCHES artist bio states "364 shows in 2 years"; all other sources give 365 — [5]
- CHVRCHES began touring as an unsigned act with online buzz approximately 10 months before releasing The Bones of What You Believe; this places the start of the touring period at approximately November 2012, consistent with the first known show in July 2012. [1]
- The TBOWYTB tour concluded in December 2014; the Under the Radar interview (published 2015-11, conducted early July 2015) states the band returned to the studio "six weeks after they ended two years of touring in December of 2014," superseding the earlier estimate of mid-November 2014 derived from a Pitchfork reference. [1, 2]
- In the wake of The Bones of What You Believe, CHVRCHES undertook seven tours of the US within the space of two years. [3]
- CHVRCHES performed at Coachella in front of approximately 35,000 people using the same minimal setup from their early touring period, and without a monitor engineer: "I think we ended up doing Coachella in front of 35,000 people with that same setup, without a monitor engineer, which is really bonkers when you think about it." (Cook) [4]
- During early touring, CHVRCHES used old Macbooks with optical drives that would skip when hit with heavy sub-bass from the PA. Cook: "It went wrong a lot as well! It really did fuck up a lot, particularly when it was hot or dusty, because we used the old Macbooks with the optical drives in them. As soon as you have a big thump of sub next to the laptop, it skips." The solution was to place slabs under the laptops to absorb the shock. [4]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Robert Smith's Teenage Cancer Trust, Royal Albert Hall, 27 March 2026
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Robert Smith's Teenage Cancer Trust concert series at the Royal Albert Hall on 2026-03-27 — described by NME as the band's first performance together in "almost three years," and by Rolling Stone as "the first time in nearly three years." [1, 2]
- At the performance, CHVRCHES debuted a new song titled "Conman," marking the track's world premiere live performance and the first public preview of material from their forthcoming fifth studio album. [1, 2]
- Lauren Mayberry made the following stage announcement introducing "Conman": "I promise I'm not just inside watching telly all the time, we have been making a record. And we thought, since this is a special night, we would play something off that record for you guys. So be kind to us, we've never played this in front of human beings before." [1, 2]
- Robert Smith served as curator of the 2026 Teenage Cancer Trust concert series at the Royal Albert Hall, stepping in for The Who's Roger Daltrey who had previously held the curatorial role. [1]
- The 2026 Teenage Cancer Trust concert series ran for seven nights at Royal Albert Hall (2026-03-23 to 2026-03-29). Full schedule as announced: 2026-03-23 — Elbow + MRCY; 2026-03-24 — comedy night; 2026-03-25 — Mogwai + Craven Faults; 2026-03-26 — Manic Street Preachers + The Joy Formidable (date inferred; article places them after March 25 and before March 27); 2026-03-27 — My Bloody Valentine + CHVRCHES ("stripped back" support); 2026-03-28 — Garbage + Placebo ("stripped back" support); 2026-03-29 — Wolf Alice + Nilüfer Yanya. [1,4]
- Robert Smith issued the following statement on curating the 2026 TCT run: "Teenage Cancer Trust does absolutely amazing work, and I am very proud they asked me to be 'Cureator' of their March 2026 concerts at the RAH. I wanted to make it a truly unforgettable, unmissable week – a run of shows to dream about – and I am so grateful to all the artists who accepted my invitation to perform. These will be very special events; every band, both headliners and special guests, and every comedian too, is either legendary or at the top of their game… indeed in most cases, they are both! It is going to be a fabulous seven nights, and I can hardly wait to experience it all. See you there!" [1]
- CHVRCHES performed in a "stripped back" fashion as support act to My Bloody Valentine on 2026-03-27; Consequence's pre-show announcement described them as opening "in a 'stripped back' fashion." Mayberry elaborated in a Billboard interview: "We can't open for My Bloody Valentine on acoustic guitars, you know? We all love the band so much and want to do right by them, and the charity." [3,4]
- The Teenage Cancer Trust annual concert series at Royal Albert Hall began in 2000; the 2026 run was its 26th edition. [4]

Caption: CHVRCHES Music Featured in "Tell Me Lies" TV Series Finale
Facts:
- CHVRCHES contributed two original cover recordings to Season 3 of the TV series *Tell Me Lies* (Hulu/Disney+): a cover of Robert Palmer's "Addicted to Love" for the Season 3 premiere (Australian commercial single released 2026-01-16) and a cover of The Postal Service's "Such Great Heights" for the Season 3 finale (Australian commercial single released 2026-02-17). [1,2]
- Both covers were recorded during the studio sessions for CHVRCHES' forthcoming fifth studio album. [2]
- The sync opportunity arose because Lauren Mayberry's personal friend Liz Elverenli joined *Tell Me Lies* as a Season 3 writer and texted Mayberry from set, asking whether CHVRCHES would be interested in doing music for the season premiere; the show's team had been discussing cover songs and artists that would connect with their viewing demographic when CHVRCHES' name came up. Elverenli then put Mayberry in touch with showrunner Meaghan Oppenheimer, who spoke with Mayberry on the phone about what they were looking for. [2]
- Mayberry stated: "It was such a treat to get to make music for the season premiere and the season finale of this show. Bookending the insanity and the heartbreak of the season three arc was an honour. 'Such Great Heights' is basically a perfect song so we were excited and terrified to cover it in equal measure." [2]
- Prior to the finale airing on Hulu, showrunner Meaghan Oppenheimer confirmed on Instagram that the show was concluding with season 3: "This was always the ending my writing team and I had in mind, and we are insanely proud of it." [2]
- Mayberry described the character Lucy in *Tell Me Lies* as someone she has "a lot of empathy" for despite poor decisions: "I think this season really shows the longer term damage that is done by those kinds of relationships — at any age but especially when someone is so young." [2]
- The college sections of *Tell Me Lies* are set in 2008 and 2009; Mayberry noted in the interview that she was also at university during that same period. [2]
- *Tell Me Lies* Season 3 premiered on Hulu and Hulu with Disney+ on 2026-01-16 with three episodes; subsequent episodes dropped weekly on Tuesdays. [3]

Caption: "Tether" (Song, The Bones of What You Believe)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- "Tether" started with guitar chords Martin Doherty wrote on guitar — open-string droning, simple shapes — before Iain Cook developed it. [1]
- The original lyrics for "Tether" were a Twin Peaks concept piece by Doherty; the pre-chorus was specifically about Laura Palmer: Lauren Mayberry quoted the original lines as "Wrapped in plastic / Why'd you water the dead flowers?" The cooler, creepier lines from the original Laura Palmer lyrics were retained in the final version; the rest was replaced by Mayberry's rewrite. [1]
- The trance breakdown at the end of "Tether" was conceived as a joke by Cook and Doherty: they were "taking the piss out of trance music." Doherty specifically referenced the UK "Euphoria"-branded compilation CDs of hardcore trance as an influence: "I loved them. When I hear trance records, even the ones people think are the corniest, some of them still get me in the feelings." [1]
- The trance arpeggiator element in "Tether"'s breakdown was a "$20 VST synth with a funny little arpeggiator on it." [1]
- The "Tether" harmonies were accidentally pitched to the wrong interval, creating an unusual dissonance: Cook stated "I feel like we accidentally pitched them up to the wrong interval, and it created this really weird dissonance." He then rounded off a few of the notes to make the vocal work but deliberately kept the weird intervals. [1]

Caption: The Leaving — "Ultimate Buzz" Debut Album (2026)
Facts:
- Martin Doherty (of CHVRCHES) and Jonny Scott formed The Leaving as a side project after both found themselves living in Los Angeles and "going through difficult times"; the project was launched in early 2026 (described as "earlier this year" relative to the March 27, 2026 article). [1]
- NME characterises The Leaving as a "rave-indebted project." [1]
- Doherty and Scott announced The Leaving's debut album, "Ultimate Buzz," on approximately March 20, 2026 ("last week" relative to the March 27, 2026 article); the album was scheduled for release on April 24, 2026. [1]

Caption: "The Mother We Share" — Official Debut Single, November 2012
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES' official debut single, "The Mother We Share," was released in November 2012. [1]
- "The Mother We Share" was the fourth demo Cook and Doherty worked on together during the TBOWYTB sessions; Cook had "no idea that it was that much more special than any of the songs we had" until friends and family unanimously singled it out. The song was changed extensively up to release, including a structural revision where what is now the verse section was originally a post-chorus. The 10th anniversary edition of TBOWYTB includes alternate versions of "The Mother We Share." [2]
- ⚠️ Conflicts with: release date — australian-charts.com lists "The Mother We Share" as released on 2012-10-08 on the National Anthem label, predating the "November 2012" date from AllMusic by approximately five weeks. The label "National Anthem" is consistent with a pre-Vertigo release; further research may clarify whether these refer to different regional release windows. [3]
- "The Mother We Share" was written and recorded in 48 hours, using only the three synths the band owned at the time. [5,6]
- "The Mother We Share" was an online hit upon its release. [1]
- "The Mother We Share" earned CHVRCHES airplay on BBC Radio 1. [5]
- The success of "The Mother We Share" led to support slots with Passion Pit and Depeche Mode. [5]
- In July 2014, "The Mother We Share" was featured in the opening ceremony for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. [1]
- As of June 2015, the music video for "The Mother We Share" had accumulated more than 10 million views on YouTube. [4]
- After its initial release in late 2012, "The Mother We Share" was pulled from streaming services (including Spotify) so that mixer Rich Costey — known for his work with Nine Inch Nails, Rage Against the Machine, Arctic Monkeys, and Sigur Rós — could produce a new version to "toughen it up a bit" for the debut album; this was described by Glassnote's Daniel Glass. [7,10]
- Glassnote's Daniel Glass described "The Mother We Share" as "a career song." [7]
- As of 2013-09, "The Mother We Share" had sold 32,000 downloads. [7]
- The band adopted the "Chvrches" orthography — replacing the "u" in the original "Churches" spelling with a "v" — concurrent with the release of "The Mother We Share" in approximately autumn 2012; prior to this single, the name had been spelled "Churches" (without the 'v'), as used at the time of posting "Lies" in May 2012. [8]
- "The Mother We Share" was certified Silver in the UK by the BPI on 2017-11-24, and certified Gold on 2024-08-30. [9]
- The BPI lists "The Mother We Share" with a release date of 2013-09-14 on UNIVERSAL MUSIC VIRGIN — distinct from the 2012 original releases on National Anthem (per australian-charts.com) and AllMusic; this corresponds to the commercial single registration on the major label imprint concurrent with The Bones of What You Believe's UK release. [9]

Caption: "Under the Tide" US Single (2014)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- "Under the Tide" was the current US single for CHVRCHES as of October 2014, confirmed by Lauren Mayberry at ACL Festival. [1]
- Martin Doherty is the primary lead vocalist on "Under the Tide" on both the album recording and in live performance; Lauren Mayberry described her own role on the track as "a little bit of keyboard, a little bit of backing vocals." Doherty's live performance of the song is characterised by an emphatic, unrestrained style; he described it: "I think it's the only way I know how. I have a lot of energy to expend because I don't sing for the whole show... It's like a football player coming off the bench at 70 minutes in a match. I'm trying to make some sort of impact." [1, 2]
- Mayberry wrote the lyrics for "Under the Tide"; Doherty sang it because the track was in a key too awkward for Mayberry — "It was in a weird key for you. So instead of repitching the whole thing, I sang it and we thought it was actually cool and we should keep it that way. It wasn't a preplanned thing." (Doherty) [3]
- Iain Cook described "Under the Tide" as a dancey song with a dynamic structure — laid-back verses that give way to punchy choruses — and noted it was effective at getting crowds jumping live. [1]

Caption: Vicious Creature — Writing and Recording Sessions
Facts:
- The album's original working title was "Fiction" — taken from the phrase "a work of fiction" that Mayberry wanted to use for the project (and which became the title of a track on the final album). CHVRCHES manager Campbell McNeil discouraged the title, arguing it would be difficult to find via Google ("music Fiction with the label and where") and that he did not want audiences to perceive the content as fictional. BBC described the Fiction incarnation as intended to be "dark, theatrical and character driven." [1,2]
- Mayberry stated she did not want to "make a crap knock-off Chvrches record," and in recording sessions would flinch whenever a collaborator pulled out a vintage synth; instead she pursued a more organic, lyrics-first approach. [2]
- Before launching her solo career, Mayberry re-signed a record deal with her CHVRCHES bandmates, assuring the group's continuity. She stated: "I was conscious it would give people a sense of security, that I'd made a commitment." [2]
- Creative director Pippa oversaw the visual aesthetic of the album and its associated campaign. Mayberry stated she was deliberate about trying to work with more women on the project, particularly on the aesthetic and photographic side. [1]
- The original album cover and the intended single cover were swapped during production — the image shot to be the album cover was reassigned as a single cover, and the intended single cover became the album cover. [1]
- Mayberry described the album's overall aesthetic direction as "Gothic, glam, feminine and evil." [1]
- Dan (Mayberry's principal co-writer, present at the 2024-12-08 Q&A) co-wrote four songs for the album, all of which appear on the final record — a 4-for-4 result. Mayberry described the collaboration as a new writing partnership: "we'd never written together before, so we were like, we'll give it a crack, see what happens." [1]
- Writing collaborators named by Mayberry at the 2024-12-08 Q&A: Dan, Matthew Koma (referred to as "Matt"), Ethan Gruska, Tobias Jesso Jr., and Greg Kurstin. Mayberry noted there may be others she did not list. [1]
- Mayberry described ending up with more collaborators than she originally anticipated: "I hadn't thought there would be that many collaborators on it, but sometimes life just has other plans." [1]
- Some songs were written early in the sessions (roughly 18 months before the album's release); others were written as late as May (year not specified in transcript, context suggests 2024), at a point when Mayberry feared the album was not coming together. She described thinking "the album's fucked" before writing several of the later tracks that resolved it. [1]
- Mayberry described the CHVRCHES songwriting process by contrast: "most of the time it is like the melody and the song are linked already, and then I'll do the lyrics later" — indicating that on Vicious Creature she had greater control over both melody and lyric from the outset. [1]

Caption: Vicious Creature (Album Release)
Facts:
- Vicious Creature is Lauren Mayberry's debut solo album, released in December 2024 via Island Records. [1, 2]
- The album title "Vicious Creature" is taken from a lyric Mayberry had written years before the album's release: "Nostalgia is such a vicious creature / Another way to say that you fear the future." [6]
- Some reviews called the album "a masterclass in pop alchemy"; others said Mayberry "still sounds like someone finding their feet." [6]
- Mayberry described her motivation for starting the solo project: "When I started the solo idea, it wasn't even about music coming out. It was more about needing to get some self-confidence and self-respect. I needed to prove to myself that within three hours, you can technically write a song." She also reflected: "I've been in a band for 12 years, and I've never worked with anybody outside of that." [3]
- Matthew Koma, a collaborator on the Vicious Creature sessions, posted on Instagram: "I'd call it a solo era but that suggests some sort of temporary stay, and instead I think we're seeing an artist beginning their most prolific years yet." [3]
- In 2023, Mayberry publicly announced she was embarking on a solo career; Rolling Stone characterises this as her "stepping away from the band" at that time. [2]
- As of October 2023, Mayberry described the debut solo album as nearly complete but unfinished: "mostly baked, it's just not here yet," and separately: "I would say it's there or thereabouts" — adding that she planned to "go home and finish it at the end of this run of touring." [4, 7]
- Mayberry described her solo vocal approach as treating the voice as instrumentation and rhythm rather than melody: "Rather than being like a vocal melody, it can be part of the instrumentation, it can be part of all the other parts of the song, it can be part of the rhythm." She added: "I like the idea of not having to have a pretty vocal — you can just push your voice to do strange things to make the song." [7]
- Mayberry described the creative genesis of the album as writing material she "couldn't or wouldn't write in the band" — specifically, material written purely from a female perspective: "These are things that I just need to say from my point of view. Obviously I'm the narrator in Chvrches, but you're still speaking on behalf of a group." [4]
- Mayberry described unproductive collaborative writing sessions that produced material indistinguishable from CHVRCHES: "The worst writing sessions were when I went in with someone who didn't really understand what I was talking about and we would just write a sort of shit Chvrches song... There's already a universe where I can make that and I enjoy it. I don't see why I would want to make the Tesco own brand version of that." [4]
- Mayberry named Tori Amos, Fiona Apple, and PJ Harvey as lifelong influences when discussing the solo project's artistic direction (NPR, September 2023): "The stuff that I've always loved is things like Tori Amos or Fiona Apple or PJ Harvey." In October 2023 she elaborated on these influences as an aesthetic moodboard also including Annie Lennox, characterising all of them as artists who "have beautiful voices but they use them quite weirdly"; she also specifically cited PJ Harvey's use of her physical self as part of the art: "I love that PJ Harvey always uses her physical self so much as part of the art." [4, 5, 7]
- Mayberry intended the solo live show to be "more theatrical" than the pre-existing CHVRCHES format, framing the theatrical approach as a way to create distance between the stage persona and the everyday self: "the thing that helped me find comfort was leaning into the more theatrical and silly things so it is completely removed from your normal self in some ways." She added that the show contained elements "that those guys [Iain and Martin] would be horrified by," and described the freedom of working outside a band format: "It's been fun to think of things that are more theatrical and not having to fit into a structure that's not necessarily designed for you to be in." [4, 7]

Caption: Vicious Creature Tour (Lauren Mayberry)
Facts:
- The Vicious Creature Tour was Lauren Mayberry's second solo tour and included a stop at the 9:30 Club in Washington, D.C. on 2025-02-18. [1]
- Mayberry toured in support of Vicious Creature in 2025. [2]
- The tour began with a brief UK instore week in December 2024; this instore leg featured only Mayberry and her principal collaborator Dan in a stripped-back format. The first stop was the Òran Mór Q&A in Glasgow on 2024-12-08. [3]
- As of 2024-12-08, announced spring 2025 shows included Glasgow Barrowlands and La Belle Angèle in Edinburgh. Mayberry noted the spring 2025 setup would feature "loud drums and synths and guitar and bass and everything," contrasting with the stripped-back instore format. [3]

Caption: "Violent Delights" (Song)
Facts:
- "Violent Delights" was inspired by The Prodigy, per the context of Martin Doherty's discussion of Screen Violence's production approach in an August 2021 interview. [1]
- At the time of the August 2021 Screen Violence promo period, Mayberry anticipated air drumming the song's explosive breakdown when CHVRCHES eventually got to perform the songs live. [1]
- The Skinny (August 2021) noted that Mayberry's voice is "pushed to its raw limits" on "Violent Delights." [1]
- Iain Cook named "Violent Delights" as one of his favourite songs to play live during the Screen Violence tour. The chorus is technically difficult for stand-in keyboard players to replicate; Mayberry noted they would say: "That fuckin' bit in the chorus of 'Violent Delights,' I can't get it!" [2]
- "Violent Delights" received its first-ever live performance at the opening show of the Screen Violence North American tour at White Oak Music Hall, Houston, TX (approximately 2021-11-13). [3]

- The set included at least: 'He Said She Said' (opener), 'Forever', 'How Not To Drown', 'Final Girl', 'Science/Visions', 'Asking For A Friend', 'The Mother We Share', and 'Clearest Blue' (partial setlist). [1]
- The stage featured a large backdrop screen showing synchronized visual content: a slow-motion drowning sequence accompanied 'How Not To Drown', and blood-red trees in a shadowy forest accompanied 'Final Girl'. [1]
- Mayberry had approximately four costume changes across the set; during the encore she appeared theatrically covered in fake blood. [1]
- As of the review's publication date (2023-06-14), CHVRCHES were next scheduled to perform at Glastonbury following these Barrowland shows. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at MTelus, Montreal Jazz Fest, 6 July 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at MTelus in Montreal on 2019-07-06 as part of the 40th Montreal International Jazz Fest. [1]
- The show was described as "their first headlining Montreal show in six years," placing the previous headlining Montreal show around 2013 (corroborated by the 99Scenes backstage Montreal interview, which documents a CHVRCHES show in Montreal in 2013). [1]
- The set opened with "Get Out" from Love Is Dead (2018). [1]
- The encore featured "The Mother We Share," during which the crowd enthusiastically sang along to the "oh oh" backing vocals throughout the track. [1]
- The set drew primarily from Love Is Dead, with songs from Every Open Eye (2015) and The Bones of What You Believe (2013) also featured; the total running time was just over an hour. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Support Passion Pit on UK Tour, Autumn 2012
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- US indie pop band Passion Pit invited CHVRCHES to support them on a UK tour after becoming converts to the band's online buzz in 2012. [1]
- At the time of the first night of the Passion Pit UK tour, CHVRCHES had played only nine shows in total. [1]
- The first night of the Passion Pit UK tour was at Dublin's Olympia Theatre, playing to a sold-out crowd; the experience was described by the band as "terrifying." [1]
- Cook explained the terror in terms of Mayberry's transition from behind instruments to centre stage: "In all her previous projects, she has had something to hide behind. She played drums in a couple of bands and then she was behind a keyboard. Now she's the singer." Cook added: "She is good with a tambourine but you can't hide behind a tambourine. Although I suppose you can channel the spectre of Stevie Nicks..." [1]
- Glassnote's Daniel Glass attended one of CHVRCHES' Passion Pit support shows during his November 2012 UK trip (three shows total: two solo CHVRCHES shows and one Passion Pit support); this was the visit that preceded the Glassnote signing announcement in January 2013. [2]
- CHVRCHES celebrated the end of the Passion Pit tour with a smaller headline show at London's Electrowerkz — a venue small enough that Mayberry "could see into the toilets from the stage." [1]
- At the Electrowerkz headline show, Mayberry performed a cover of Prince's "I Would Die 4 U," which had rapidly become a staple of the band's live set. Cook said: "Prince is one musical touchstone that we all have." The cover remained in use through at least June 2013, closing the sold-out Granada Theater, Dallas show on 2013-06-06. [1,3]

Caption: "Make Them Gold" (Song — Every Open Eye)

Caption: CHVRCHES at Sasquatch! Music Festival, George, Washington, 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES made a "signature appearance" at Sasquatch during the TBOWYTB touring period (2013–2014). [1]
- Sasquatch! Music Festival is held in George, Washington; Mayberry described the 2013 edition as "one of the first US festivals Chvrches played." [2]
- The Postal Service were playing Sasquatch 2013 as part of the tenth anniversary of the album *Give Up*; Mayberry noted it "felt like every other band on the line-up stayed to watch" The Postal Service perform. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Reading & Leeds Festival (TBOWYTB Era)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES made a "signature appearance" at Reading & Leeds during the TBOWYTB touring period (2013–2014). [1]
- CHVRCHES performed at Reading Festival on 2013-08-23 and at Leeds Festival on 2013-08-24, per the June 2013 tour announcement. [2]

Caption: "Playing Dead" (Song)
Facts: - The SXSW 2016 official CHVRCHES artist bio characterises "Playing Dead" (alongside "Bury It" and "Never Ending Circles") as being about "being unapologetic and not being told what to do or who to be." This represents the band's/label's own promotional framing of the song's lyrical theme. [1]

Caption: "Clearest Blue" (Song)
Facts:
- The album title Every Open Eye is taken from a line in "Clearest Blue." [1]
- The SXSW 2016 official CHVRCHES artist bio characterises "Clearest Blue" (alongside "Down Side Of Me" and "Afterglow") as being about "moving on to better things, holding on to the good and letting go of the bad." This represents the band's/label's own promotional framing of the song's lyrical theme. [2]
- "Clearest Blue" was released as a commercial single in Australia on 2016-02-29 on the Goodbye / Vertigo / Capitol imprint. [3]

Caption: "Graves" (Song)
Facts:
- "Graves," a track on Love Is Dead, contains a lyrical reference to Alan Kurdi, the three-year-old Syrian boy whose body was found on a Turkish beach in 2015 after his family's attempt to cross to Europe by sea. Mayberry's lyrics include: "They're leaving bodies in stairwells and washing up on the shores." [1]
- Mayberry described the lyrical influence of the Alan Kurdi tragedy on "Graves" as natural and unforced: "when we're writing, we don't really sit down and think, this is the theme of the song… I think it just is. It feels natural in that time and when we were making the record." She also connected the song's political content to growing older: "writing anti-love songs doesn't necessarily feel as relevant to me as it did when I was 23." [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at BBC Radio 1's Biggest Weekend, Swansea, 2018-05-26
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at BBC Radio 1's Biggest Weekend in Swansea on 2018-05-26, the day after the release of Love Is Dead (2018-05-25). [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES — BBC Radio 1 Session for Huw Stephens at Maida Vale Studios, 2012
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES recorded a session for Huw Stephens' show on BBC Radio 1, held at Maida Vale Studios in London; based on a Breaking More Waves blog post dated 2012-11-08 that references the session as happening "this week," the session took place in approximately 2012-11. [1,2]
- Martin Doherty described the Maida Vale session as "a really, really big deal for me," stating it "kind of lived up to the hype." He had grown up listening to Steve Lamacq and John Peel, both of whom regularly featured Maida Vale sessions; recording one was a goal he had held since his teenage years. [1]
- The session included at least "We Sink" and "Science/Visions" (referred to as "Science & Vision" in the Breaking More Waves post); the latter was characterised as having "electroclash pulses." [2]
- "We Sink" was performed with male lead vocals at this session, described by the Breaking More Waves reviewer as "featuring male rather than their normal lead female vocals." [2]

Caption: 7-Inches for Planned Parenthood (Charity Boxset, 2017)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES contributed to the "7-Inches for Planned Parenthood" charity vinyl boxset, organized by Brandon Stosuy (The Creative Independent) and Matt Berninger of The National, with proceeds going to Planned Parenthood (described by the project website as approximately half the gross profits). [1,2]
- CHVRCHES' contribution was a new live version of "Down Side of Me"; an accompanying video directed by Kristen Stewart was released on 2017-04-28 as the collection's first single. [1,2]
- The boxset was announced as available for pre-order on 2017-04-28. [1]
- The project was conceived in November [2016] when Brandon Reid suggested to Matt Berninger pulling together artists for a series of 7" vinyl records benefiting progressive institutions; Planned Parenthood was their first choice. [1]
- Brandon Stosuy curated the music and visual art pairings for the boxset; Emily Alexander handled the literary component; Mark "Flanny" Flanagan handled comedy selections. [1]
- CHVRCHES were among approximately 30 contributors; a partial list of other contributors includes John Legend, Björk, Bon Iver, Arca, Common, Feist, Foo Fighters, Mitski, Sarah Silverman, Sharon Van Etten, Sleater-Kinney, and St. Vincent. [1,2]
- The video for "Down Side of Me (Live)" was previewed approximately one week before its official release on 2017-04-28 (teaser date approximately 2017-04-21). [2]
- The vinyl box set was priced at $100 per order; buyers from 2017-04-28 received immediate access to a digital version of the compilation, with physical vinyl expected to ship in fall 2017. [2]
- Lauren Mayberry stated in a press release dated 2017-04-28: "'Down Side Of Me' was not written specifically for this project but I really like how the lyrics can be interpreted in a way that supports the goal of the project as a whole. We should all be able to have faith that our governments are working in our best interests — and if they aren't then, they should be challenged and held to account. 'I'll believe that you're all that you said you would be.'" [2]

Caption: "Wonderland" (Song)
Facts: - "Wonderland" is the closing track of Love Is Dead (2018). It is preceded by "ii," an instrumental/spoken word intro track containing the lyric "Truth exists somewhere between the dark and the light." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Berghain, Berlin, 19 April 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Berghain, Berlin on 2013-04-19 as part of a Vertigo Berlin label night ("Label-Abend"). The show was sold out. [1]
- Arthur Beatrice supported CHVRCHES at the 2013-04-19 Berghain show. Set times: Arthur Beatrice 21:15, CHVRCHES 22:30; doors 20:00; venue age restriction 18+. [1]

Caption: A2IM Libera Awards 2014 — CHVRCHES Win Breakthrough Artist of the Year
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES won the A2IM Breakthrough Artist of the Year award for *The Bones of What You Believe* (Glassnote Records) at the third annual A2IM Libera Awards (colloquially "the Libbys"), held at the Highline Ballroom, New York City, on 2014-06-19. [1]
- At the same ceremony, Glassnote Records — CHVRCHES' label at the time — won the Label of the Year award in the category of 6 or more full-time employees worldwide. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Forecastle Festival, Louisville, Kentucky, July 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES were booked as a non-headlining act at the 17th annual Forecastle Festival in Louisville, Kentucky, held at Waterfront Park on the banks of the Ohio River, 2019-07-12 to 2019-07-14. The lineup was publicly announced on 2019-02-11. Day headliners were The Killers (Friday, 2019-07-12), Anderson .Paak & The Free Nationals (Saturday, 2019-07-13), and The Avett Brothers (Sunday, 2019-07-14). [1,2,3]
- CHVRCHES were listed specifically on the Sunday (2019-07-14) day lineup in the official Forecastle Festival press release. [2]
- Co-billed acts at the 2019 Forecastle Festival included Portugal. The Man, Maggie Rogers, Nelly, Chromeo, Moon Taxi, Tyler Childers, Judah & The Lion, First Aid Kit, and Dawes, among others. [1,2]

Caption: CHVRCHES Pitchfork Filmed Session, April 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - In April 2013, CHVRCHES filmed a session for Pitchfork; this was among their early US promotional activities ahead of the September 2013 release of The Bones of What You Believe. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES First US Tour, March 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES' first US tour kicked off in San Francisco on 2013-03-10, per a Billboard trade piece published 2013-03-04 announcing the upcoming run. [1]
- Both of CHVRCHES' 2013-03-18 shows at Mercury Lounge, New York City had already sold out at the time of the Billboard article's publication (2013-03-04), nearly two weeks before the shows. [1]
- The North American tour was planned to wrap in late March 2013, after which the band intended to return to recording their full-length debut. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Cover Janelle Monáe's "Tightrope" at Billboard's Women in Music Awards
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed a live cover of Janelle Monáe's "Tightrope" at Billboard's Women in Music Awards. The event predates 2015-02 (the date of the BBC Radio 1 "Cry Me a River" cover, which a contemporaneous Billboard article invoked the Women in Music performance as a recent precedent for); Billboard Women in Music 2014, held in December 2014, is the most likely occasion. [1]

Caption: "Really Gone" (Song)
Facts: - "Really Gone" is a track on Love Is Dead (2018) and one of three tracks on the album self-produced by CHVRCHES (not produced with Greg Kurstin); the other two self-produced tracks were "My Enemy" (feat. Matt Berninger of The National) and "God's Plan." After six months of writing and recording, Doherty said the band were able to see these three tracks "through to the end of the process." On the decision to self-produce, Doherty quipped: "It was cheap." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Governors Ball, New York City, June 2018
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Governors Ball in New York City on 2018-06-03, as part of the Love Is Dead touring cycle. [1]
- The 2018 Governors Ball appearance was framed by Doherty as a "rain check" from a 2016 booking: Governors Ball had cancelled its Sunday lineup in 2016 — including a headlining set by Kanye West — due to thunderstorms, implying CHVRCHES had been on the cancelled 2016 Sunday bill and the 2018 appearance was the rescheduled make-up date. Doherty: "I'm going to be so pissed if we get rained off again, honestly." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Support Coldplay on Global Stadium Tour, Brazil, March 2023
Facts:
- CHVRCHES were confirmed to perform a string of dates supporting Coldplay's global stadium tour in Brazil in March 2023; the booking was announced alongside the "Over" single and Island Records deal in a Billboard interview published 2023-02-23. [1]
- At the time of the 2023-02-23 Billboard interview, Mayberry cryptically added: "there's another batch of shows that are coming, at some point" — indicating additional upcoming live activity beyond the Brazil dates had not yet been publicly announced. [1]
- The Coldplay stadium tour support was completed by October 2023; the BroadwayWorld anniversary-edition press release (2023-10-20) references it as "Having completed a stadium tour with Coldplay earlier this year." [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Billboard's NYC Studio — Performance and Interview, October 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed "Recover" and "The Mother We Share" at Billboard's NYC studio in 2013-10, and sat for an interview as part of the US promotional campaign for The Bones of What You Believe. [1]
- Iain Cook on the state of the band's current US touring: "We can see the rooms getting bigger. People know the songs; the record's finally out there. It's getting to a nice level and we're enjoying ourselves." [1]
- Iain Cook stated the band was already beginning to think about new material during the TBOWYTB touring cycle: "Ultimately we want to get some time to start getting new ideas down for the new record. Things are starting to build up creatively, and it would be great to really get the ball rolling on that. We're just trying to get the time window to do it, during all the touring." [1]
- Lauren Mayberry described her philosophy on the relationship between performer and listener as a "transfer of ownership" of a song's meaning in live performance: "You see someone really enjoying the song. But they're enjoying it in a different way than you are because it means something different to them… That can be a powerful thing about lyrics." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Bangkok Show — Scheduled and Cancelled, 2014
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES were scheduled to appear in Bangkok in 2014 but cancelled unexpectedly; the cancellation is noted in a 2016 BK Magazine announcement as background context for what the article describes as the band's Bangkok debut that September. No further details — promoter, venue, or specific date — are known from this source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at BITEC Bangna Hall, Bangkok — 3 September 2016 (Bangkok Debut)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at BITEC Bangna Hall (88 Bang Na-Trat Rd.), Bangkok, Thailand on 2016-09-03, presented by Tiger Jams and Fungjai as part of the "Center Stage" event series; the show was billed as CHVRCHES' Bangkok debut. [1]
- Ticket pricing was tiered: B700 (early bird), B1,000 (regular admission), and B1,500 at the door; tickets went on sale no later than 2016-07-14, the date of the BK Magazine announcement article. [1]
- Local support acts included Polycat, Jelly Rocket, and Somkiat (described as "local indie rockers"), and Monkey Business, Mirrr, and 23'0 (described as "up and coming bands"); visual artist DJs Dudesweet, Eyedropper Fill, and Tuna Dunn also performed on the bill. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Primavera Sound, Barcelona, May 2014
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Primavera Sound, Barcelona in May 2014, during the TBOWYTB touring cycle; a Blisstopic video interview filmed at the festival features all three members — Lauren Mayberry, Martin Doherty, and Iain Cook. [1]
- Martin Doherty stated the band does not significantly change songs from the recorded versions for live performance, because festival audiences are often connecting with the album for the first time: "we don't really change the songs live too much from the record because it's a lot of places that we go to we're going to the first time and the people were just connecting with the album for the first time so we try to deliver the album relatively close to what is on the record." [1]
- Iain Cook stated his opposition to artists completely reinventing their music for live performance, citing the experience of first-time audience members who paid to hear their favourite songs as they know them: "always pissing me off when artists take upon themselves to completely reinvent their music for live because you know it's one sense all of people are seeing for the first time and they paid to hear their favorite songs and then just completely fuck it up." [1]
- Cook cited seeing Prince play "a blues rock version of Let's Go Crazy" as an example of this, saying "This isn't the song I know and love." The remark was made at Primavera Sound 2014 on 2014-05-30; Prince headlined the festival the preceding night (2014-05-29), making the "the other day" reference consistent with CHVRCHES witnessing Prince's Primavera Sound set. [1]
- Martin Doherty described the festival environment as motivating CHVRCHES to push harder to win over new fans who had not specifically bought a ticket to see them: "in a festival environment it's possible that you will be playing in front of a lot of people who've heard your name and are interested and heard other people talking about it but have never seen you playing. That encourages you to like push harder and try to win people over." [1]

Caption: Lauren Mayberry on Block FM CHATROOM, June 2018
Facts:
- Lauren Mayberry appeared on Block FM's recurrent "CHATROOM" interview segment in approximately 2018-06-11 (YouTube upload date; actual filming date may differ), in a segment titled "Block FM presents CHATROOM w/ Lauren Mayberry (CHVRCHES) — Craziest Fan Moments." [1]
- During the interview, Mayberry recounted attending a CHVRCHES show in San Francisco (specific date unknown; at some point before 2018-06-11) at which audience members dressed as "furries" — people in "big animal suits, head to toe" — were visible in the crowd, including someone costumed as a giant panda and someone costumed as a giant tiger. Mayberry: "I was like, 'Am I really jet-lagged, or is that happening?' And it was happening. I don't know why, but good for them — they were being free and being themselves." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Boiler Room Album Launch Party, London, 27 September 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at a Boiler Room event billed as the "Album Launch Party" in London on 2013-09-27, running from 19:30 to 23:00 GMT. [1]
- Alex Patchwork and Cid Rim also performed on the bill at the same event. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Cannes Lions Spotify Beach, Cannes, June 2018
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Spotify Beach on La Croisette at the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity during the week of approximately 2018-06-18 to 2018-06-22 (article published 2018-06-25, describing the festival as having just concluded); the booking appears in a trade/marketing publication and is not documented elsewhere in the corpus. [1]
- CHVRCHES performed at Spotify Beach on the same bill as rapper Travis Scott; the article describes both acts as keeping "the crowd dancing late into the night," with Travis Scott ending the night with a bar-top performance. [1]
- Spotify hosted two nights of beachside music at the festival, described as oceanside parties on Tuesday and Wednesday evenings of the festival week (approximately 2018-06-19 and 2018-06-20); The Killers performed a set of greatest hits on one of these nights. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Paraíso Festival, Madrid — 14 June 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Paraíso Festival in Madrid on 2019-06-14, as part of the Love Is Dead touring cycle; the date was confirmed by the British Council Spain interview preamble ("In anticipation of the performance of CHVRCHES at Madrid's Paraíso Festival on June 14th"), with Lauren Mayberry also describing it as "in two weeks time" from the interview conducted in late May 2019. [1]
- Mayberry noted Spain was a country the band did not get to visit as often as they would like. [1]
- Mayberry described Spanish festival crowds favourably: "the Spanish festival crowds are always really, really good fun and really up for it… it feels like real music fans who are really there for a proper experience." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Ancienne Belgique, Brussels, 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Ancienne Belgique in Brussels in 2013; five live tracks recorded at this show were included on The Bones of What You Believe 10th Anniversary Special Edition (released October 2023). [1]

Caption: Iain Cook — Solo Project "Protection"
Facts: - Iain Cook has a solo project called "Protection," active as of October 2023; it is mentioned alongside Lauren Mayberry's solo endeavour in the BroadwayWorld anniversary-edition press release (2023-10-20) as one of the band members' "various solo projects." No further details — genre, label, or release information — are available from this source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Brooklyn Bowl Las Vegas, 11 December 2021
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Brooklyn Bowl Las Vegas (The Linq Promenade, Las Vegas, NV) on Saturday, 2021-12-11; Donna Missal was billed as special guest. [1]
- The show had an 18+ age restriction; doors opened at 7:30 PM. [1]
- Ticket pricing was tiered: $31.00 General Admission (advance), $34.00 General Admission (week of show), $36.00 General Admission (day of show), and $51.00 Club Level; all prices included Nevada's 9% Live Entertainment Tax. [1]
- COVID-19 entry protocols were in effect: attendees were required to provide proof of either full vaccination (card or photo with matching ID, at least two weeks prior to the show) or a negative PCR or antigen COVID-19 test administered within 72 hours of the show date; at-home tests were not accepted; acceptable vaccines included Pfizer, Moderna, Johnson & Johnson, and WHO-authorised vaccines. [1]
- Masks were required to be worn at all times for attendees regardless of vaccination status, except while actively eating or drinking; all Brooklyn Bowl staff were fully vaccinated and masked inside the venue. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Radio City Music Hall, New York City, 11 July 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Radio City Music Hall in New York City on 2019-07-11 as part of their North American tour. [1,2]
- Charly Bliss opened for CHVRCHES at Radio City Music Hall on 2019-07-11. [1,2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Pioneer Works / Nissan Converge, Brooklyn, 12 July 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed a free show at Pioneer Works in Brooklyn on 2019-07-12 as part of Nissan's "Converge" event, billed as "a celebration and exploration of technology and creativity"; entry was free with RSVP, subject to capacity. [1,2]
- CHVRCHES were scheduled to perform at 9:00pm as the headline act at Converge, with the event running from noon to midnight. [1]
- The Nissan Converge event featured panel discussions (Future of Transportation, Technology of Racing, Designing to the Moon and Back), workshops (VRBAR Tilt Brush, Beyond Meat), keynote addresses by Allyson Witherspoon and Jen Lewin, and art installations by Dustin Yellin, Jen Lewin, Christopher Willits, and others. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Smart Araneta Coliseum, Manila, 13 August 2019
Facts:
- ⚠️ Conflicts with: [1], fact "CHVRCHES performed at Smart Araneta Coliseum in Manila, Philippines on 2019-08-13 as part of their Asian tour leg." — [1] (BrooklynVegan) pre-dated the cancellation, listing Manila as an upcoming tour date; the show was subsequently cancelled before it took place.
- CHVRCHES cancelled their planned show at Smart Araneta Coliseum in Manila, Philippines, which had been scheduled for 2019-08-13 as part of their *Love is Dead Asia Tour 2019*. The cancellation was cited as "due to unforeseen circumstances" with no further detail provided by the band. [2]
- The cancellation was announced on 2019-07-12 through the Facebook page of local promoter Ovation Productions. [2]
- CHVRCHES issued the following statement regarding the cancellation: "We'd like to apologize to all our fans that had already purchased tickets and we'll be posting full ticket refund details here very soon." [2]
- The cancelled show would have been CHVRCHES' third visit to the Philippines; their first was a solo concert in 2014, and their second was an appearance at GoodVybes Fest in 2016. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Music Festival Yoursummer, Incheon, South Korea, 15 August 2019
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Music Festival Yoursummer in Incheon, South Korea on 2019-08-15 as part of their Asian tour leg. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Summer Sonic 2019, Osaka and Tokyo
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Summer Sonic in Osaka, Japan on 2019-08-17 and in Tokyo, Japan on 2019-08-18 as part of their Asian tour leg. [1]
- The 2019-08-18 Tokyo Summer Sonic show became the final date of CHVRCHES' Asian tour leg; their subsequent show at AsiaWorld-Expo, Hong Kong on 2019-08-20 was cancelled due to pro-democracy protests disrupting Hong Kong's airport operations. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at AsiaWorld-Expo, Hong Kong, 20 August 2019 (Cancelled)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at AsiaWorld-Expo in Sha Tau Kok Tan Ka, Hong Kong on 2019-08-20 as part of their Asian tour leg. [1]
  - ⚠️ Conflicts with: [2], which is an AP wire report published 2019-08-15 — five days before the scheduled show — confirming the concert was cancelled due to pro-democracy protests that forced Hong Kong's airport to shut down. Source [1] (BrooklynVegan, published before the cancellation) listed Hong Kong as an upcoming tour date; the original extractor appears to have incorrectly rendered this as a confirmed performance.
- CHVRCHES cancelled their scheduled 2019-08-20 concert at AsiaWorld-Expo in Hong Kong, citing "unforeseen circumstances"; the cancellation was announced on 2019-08-13, two days before the AP wire report published on 2019-08-15. [2]
- The cancelled Hong Kong show was to have been CHVRCHES' first solo concert in Hong Kong. [2]
- The cancellation occurred amid pro-democracy protests that forced Hong Kong's airport to shut down operations for a second day; at least three overseas performer concerts were cancelled in the same period, including shows by K-pop act Kang Daniel and American singer-songwriter Alec Benjamin. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Reading Festival, Reading, UK, 25 August 2019

Caption: CHVRCHES at B.R. Cohn Winery, Glen Ellen, CA, 21 September 2019
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at B.R. Cohn Winery and Olive Oil Company in Glen Ellen, California on 2019-09-21 as part of their North American fall tour. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Union Market, Washington, DC, 12 October 2019
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Union Market in Washington, DC on 2019-10-12 as part of their fall North American tour. [1]
- The show was the closing act of Day 1 of the sixth annual All Things Go Fall Classic, a two-day festival held at Union Market's Dock 5; CHVRCHES headlined the Saturday night as the final act of the evening. [2]
- Mayberry described the set from the stage as "a celebration of sadness, rather than the sadness itself." [2]
- Lauren Mayberry wore a glittery silver jumpsuit on stage, which she joked made her look like "someone's fancy nan." [2]
- Mayberry made an on-stage feminist remark: "actually, if I was a rock band guy I could try 40% less hard...I could just roll out of bed and they'd be like, 'genius!'" [2]
- There was approximately one hour of dead time between the end of the penultimate act LANY's set (LANY performed at 8:30 PM) and CHVRCHES taking the stage. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at O2 Academy Birmingham, 18 March 2022
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at O2 Academy Birmingham on 2022-03-18 as part of the Screen Violence UK tour. [1]
- Support was provided by HighSchool. [1]
- The stage featured a large screen displaying static and grainy movie footage throughout the set. [1]
- Lauren Mayberry opened the show wearing a red sparkly high-shouldered outfit; she joked from the stage that it had been causing her difficulties going through doorways backstage. [1]
- The set included at least: "He Said She Said" (opener), "How Not To Drown," "Forever," "California," "Science/Visions," "Good Girls," "Bury It," "Night Sky," "Final Girl," "Recover," "Never Say Die" (main set closer); encore: "Asking For A Friend," "The Mother We Share," "Clearest Blue." Partial — the exact order of the middle-set songs is not fully confirmed. [1]
- Iain Cook and Martin Doherty also moved to the front of the stage and played guitar during songs including "Forever" and "California." [1]
- For "Final Girl," Mayberry left the stage to change into a white t-shirt bearing the song's title, signalling the approach of the set's closing act. [1]
- Mayberry encouraged the audience to sing along during "Never Say Die" before the band left the stage to close the main set. [1]
- Mayberry returned for the encore covered in fake blood, completing the slasher-film visual concept associated with Screen Violence. [1]
- Mayberry described Screen Violence from the stage as "a very miserable record." [1]
- Robert Smith was not present in Birmingham; Mayberry revealed from the stage: "We're on the second day of the worst hangovers of our lives because Robert Smith wanted to take us out drinking and how do you say no to that?" — a reference to Robert Smith having made an on-stage guest appearance at the band's previous show at Brixton Academy, London on 2022-03-16, followed by a night out. [1]
- During "How Not To Drown," Martin Doherty played a small piano brought down closer to the front of the stage, while Mayberry managed both vocal parts of the duet (her own and Smith's studio part). [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Falls Festival 2022/2023
Facts:
- CHVRCHES were confirmed in the Falls Festival 2022/2023 lineup, announced on 2022-05-04, scheduled across three Australian sites: Pennyroyal Plains, Colac VIC (29–31 December 2022); North Byron Parklands, Yelgun NSW (31 December 2022, 1–2 January 2023); and Fremantle Park, Fremantle WA (7–8 January 2023). CHVRCHES' specific performance date(s) within each site's run are not confirmed by this source. [1]
- CHVRCHES were billed in the top-tier El Capitan Stage headliner bloc alongside Arctic Monkeys, Lil Nas X, Peggy Gou, and Jamie xx. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Columbiahalle, Berlin, approximately April 2014
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Columbiahalle, Berlin at some point in approximately 2014-04; established by the B.Z. Berlin review of their 2016-04-06 Columbiahalle show, which notes "almost exactly two years ago this band stood on this exact stage." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Lollapalooza Berlin, September 2015
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Lollapalooza in Berlin in 2015; a B.Z. Berlin concert review from 2016-04-07 notes that the Columbiahalle show was where Berlin fans could hear CHVRCHES' Every Open Eye material in a full concert setting, having previously first encountered the new songs at Lollapalooza. (Source is a German-language article — "beim Lollapalooza Festival" — without specifying Berlin; the Berlin context of the review makes this the most probable referent, as Lollapalooza Berlin launched its inaugural edition in September 2015.) [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Columbiahalle, Berlin, 6 April 2016
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Columbiahalle, Berlin on 2016-04-06 (a Wednesday evening). [1]
- Support was provided by Shura, a London-based artist, who performed a 40-minute set; live material was described as including notably rockier tracks not yet on any release, some of which ended in a "noise wall." [1]
- CHVRCHES opened their set at 21:20 with "Never Ending Circles." [1]
- The stage featured flashing LED walls and a precisely coordinated light show. [1]
- Between songs, Lauren Mayberry told anecdotes about her German language skills. [1]
- The set included at least: "Never Ending Circles" (opener), "Empty Threat," "Gun," "Clearest Blue" (main set closer); encore: "The Mother We Share" against a glittering backdrop. (Partial setlist.) [1]
- Martin Doherty and Iain Cook were positioned at bass, synthesizer, and sampler during the performance. [1]
- "Empty Threat" prompted the first large crowd reaction of the night. [1]
- During "Gun," Lauren Mayberry had a laughing fit — including what the reviewer described as an involuntary "cute grunt" — and could barely hold herself together through the rest of the song. (Source is a German-language review; this is a translation.) [1]
- This was CHVRCHES' second performance at Columbiahalle, Berlin; the first had been approximately two years prior (circa 2014-04). [1]

Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed a solo concert in the Philippines in 2014; this was their first visit to the country. No further details — venue, city, or exact date — are confirmed from this source. [1]

Facts: - CHVRCHES appeared at GoodVybes Fest in the Philippines in 2016; this was their second visit to the country. No further details — venue, exact date, or performance notes — are confirmed from this source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES in Seattle, 30 September 2022 (Screen Violence World Tour)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed in Seattle on 2022-09-30 as part of the Screen Violence World Tour; the venue is not named in this source. [1]
- Support was provided by Cafuné, a synth-pop duo known for their viral TikTok hit "Tek It." [1]
- Mayberry wore an all-black outfit with glitter tears painted on her face. [1]
- The set included at least: "He Said She Said" (partial setlist — only one song title named in this source). [1]
- At concert open, the GA floor was packed and upper balcony seats were filled. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at 9:30 Club, Washington DC (Three Nights, June 2022)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed three consecutive nights at the 9:30 Club, Washington DC on 2022-06-05, 2022-06-06, and 2022-06-07 as part of the Screen Violence tour. [1]
- A setlist.fm page exists for at least one of the three DC shows; the specific night it documents is not identified by this source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES in Charlotte, approximately 4 June 2022 (Screen Violence Tour)
Facts: - CHVRCHES played a show in Charlotte on approximately 2022-06-04, immediately before a three-night run at the 9:30 Club in Washington DC (2022-06-05–07); the date is inferred from a fan account stating they left Charlotte after the show to catch a 7:13 am flight in order to reach DC in time for the first DC show. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Fat Sam's, Dundee — Cancelled (Screen Violence UK Tour Opening, March 2022)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES were scheduled to open their Screen Violence UK tour with two intimate shows in Dundee and Glasgow; the Dundee show was held at Fat Sam's and was scheduled for approximately 2022-03-08. The Dundee show was cancelled after two members of CHVRCHES tested positive for COVID-19. [1]
- Tickets for both the Dundee (Fat Sam's) and Glasgow intimate shows were sold exclusively via Assai Records in-store bundle purchases. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Fat Sam's, Dundee, 12 June 2023 (Final Screen Violence Headline Show)
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Fat Sam's, Dundee on 2023-06-12; the show was characterised by the fan authors as the final Screen Violence headline show, framing it as a full-circle bookend to the cancelled March 2022 show at the same venue that had been intended to open the UK leg. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Metro, Chicago — 24 October 2015
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Metro (3730 N. Clark St., Chicago, IL) at 9 p.m. on 2015-10-24 (Saturday); the show was sold out (listed as a "sold out" show in the Chicago Tribune concert listings, published 2015-10-22). [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Central Park, New York — September 2015
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed a show at Central Park in New York in September 2015; during the performance, Lauren Mayberry confronted a male fan who had been shouting "Marry me!" at her from the audience. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Canadian Musicfest, Mod Club, Toronto, 20 March 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at the Mod Club in Toronto on 2013-03-20 as part of Canadian Musicfest; this was one of a few select North American dates the band played around South by Southwest 2013. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES "In Search Of Darkness" Playlist Post — March 2021
Facts:
- On 2021-03-19, CHVRCHES posted on their official Instagram account sharing a fan-curated playlist titled "In Search Of Darkness," described in the caption as a collection of "your top suggestions for most-haunted CHVRCHES track." The post directed followers to "listen at the link in bio." [1]
- The post predates the first Screen Violence single "He Said She Said" (Australian release 2021-04-19) by approximately one month, and the formal Screen Violence album announcement (2021-06-03) by approximately two and a half months — establishing that CHVRCHES were publicly cultivating a horror/darkness aesthetic associated with the SV era as early as March 2021. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES / John Carpenter 7-Inch on Sacred Bones Records, August 2021
Facts:
- CHVRCHES collaborated with John Carpenter and Sacred Bones Records on a 7-inch release featuring two tracks: "Good Girls (John Carpenter Remix)" and CHVRCHES' remix of Carpenter's song "Turning The Bones." [1]
- Both tracks were made available to stream on 2021-08-06, the date of the collaboration announcement; the 7-inch physical release was in pre-order at time of announcement and had not yet shipped. [1]
- CHVRCHES described the collaboration as an honour: "Honoured to have collaborated with the legendary @johncarpenterofficial and @SacredBones on a new 7-inch." [1]

Caption: The Bones of What You Believe — 10th Anniversary Edition (2023)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES announced the TBOWYTB 10th Anniversary Edition on 2023-09-07 via their official Instagram account, with a release date of 2023-10-20. [1]
- The 10th Anniversary Edition includes 4 unreleased songs originally recorded for The Bones of What You Believe. [1,2]
- The 10th Anniversary Edition includes 5 live tracks from 2013 recorded at AB Brussels (a venue in Brussels, Belgium). [1,2]
- The 10th Anniversary Edition is available on vinyl, CD, and digital formats. [1,2]
- Pre-orders for the anniversary edition were available as of 2023-09-07. [1]
- CHVRCHES stated via Instagram (2023-09-07): "It feels quite strange that Bones is almost a decade old — in some ways, it's like it just happened, but also like that era was a lifetime ago. Thank you to all the people who gave that album a special place in their heart, and still show us so much kindness today." [1]
- As of February 2023 — approximately seven months before the formal anniversary announcement (2023-09-07) — the band had already begun going through archive hard drives in search of unreleased material. Mayberry stated: "We're in the process of digging through all the hard drives, seeing if there are any treats... if we can find any weird janky demos, then we will share them"; Cook confirmed they would likely be "janky." [3]

Caption: Every Open Eye — Record Store Day 2025 Double Vinyl Reissue
Facts:
- CHVRCHES released a special double vinyl reissue of Every Open Eye for Record Store Day 2025 on 2025-04-12; the release was announced on 2025-02-06 via the band's official Instagram account. [1]
- CHVRCHES described Every Open Eye as "our second album" in the Record Store Day 2025 announcement. [1]
- The RSD 2025 reissue was available exclusively at participating Record Store Day shops; the band linked to recordstoreday.co.uk for store listings. [1]
- The RSD 2025 double vinyl is coloured vinyl; Iain Cook confirmed this while opening the sleeve in the unboxing video: "Let's see if it's coloured. Oh, that's nice actually." [2]
- The 10th anniversary reissue became available on streaming platforms on 2025-04-11, one day before the physical Record Store Day release on 2025-04-12; Lauren Mayberry confirmed in the unboxing video: "I think streaming today, this tomorrow." [2]
- Martin Doherty stated in the unboxing video (2025-04-11): "I also do really respect this album and I love it." [2]

Caption: "How Not to Drown" Music Video YouTube Premiere Livestream, June 2021
Facts:
- CHVRCHES hosted a YouTube premiere livestream for the "How Not to Drown" music video in June 2021 (titled "HOW NOT TO DROWN PRE-PARTY"), with all three members participating live — Lauren Mayberry and Martin Doherty from Los Angeles, Iain Cook from Scotland. [1]
- Robert Smith joined the premiere by phone call, live on stream; Martin Doherty dialled him unprompted after fans in the chat enthusiastically requested it. [1]
- During the livestream, the band screened behind-the-scenes clips from the music video shoot, footage of a remote Zoom writing/recording session, and played two audio demos: a version with the original (pre-final) lyrics and a snippet of the final vocal recording of "How Not to Drown." [1]
- The YouTube video title gives the date as "3 Jun 2021"; the digital single was released 2021-06-02 and the Screen Violence album was announced 2021-06-03, making this the day of the album announcement. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Record at Bad Robot Studios, 2018
Facts: - During the Love Is Dead touring cycle in 2018, CHVRCHES recorded at Bad Robot (the Los Angeles studio associated with J.J. Abrams' production company); Mayberry described it alongside the Hansa Studios session as "a bucket list thing for all of us." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Squamish Valley Music Festival, August 2014
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at the fifth instalment of the Squamish Valley Music Festival (formerly Live at Squamish) in Squamish, British Columbia, on the Stawamus secondary stage, on Day 2 of the three-day event. The performance date is inferred as approximately 2014-08-09 from the article's publication date of 2014-08-13 and its "this past weekend" framing. [1]
- CHVRCHES entered the stage behind a "thin veil of theatrical smoke," per the reviewer. [1]
- Notable co-billed acts at the festival included Arcade Fire (Day 2 headliner), Eminem (Day 3 headliner), Bruno Mars (Day 1 headliner), Arctic Monkeys (Day 3), and Broken Bells (Day 2). [1]

Caption: "ZVVL" (Song, Recover EP)
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- "ZVVL" appeared on the Recover EP (2013-03) as one of three original tracks; it was not included on The Bones of What You Believe. [1]
- Martin Doherty takes lead vocals on "ZVVL", trading vocal lead with Lauren Mayberry; the Consequence of Sound review describes it as "a slower number that builds momentum without ever breaking." [1]
- Lauren Mayberry confirmed the title "ZVVL" is a reference to Zuul the Gatekeeper from Ghostbusters, via the band's signature U→V letter swap; when a Facebook user asked about the song title's meaning, Mayberry responded by posting the Sigourney Weaver Ghostbusters clip. Doherty described it as "another instance of '80s cinema influence." [2]
