Caption: "Final Girl" (Song)
Facts:
- The bass part on "Final Girl" was written by Iain Cook and Martin Doherty; Cook described it as "a tricky one on bass." [1]
- Doherty performs the bass part live; it causes hand cramping due to its technical difficulty — Doherty confirmed: "my hands cramp up every night because it's so hard, but I can just about play it." [1]
- Iain Cook named "Final Girl" as one of his favourite songs to play live during the Screen Violence tour. [1]
- During the Screen Violence tour, Mayberry changed into a "Final Girl" t-shirt bearing the song's title for the performance of this song each night. [1]
- "Final Girl" imagines the perspective of a horror film survivor; Mayberry described the song's concept: after enduring all the trauma, "you know she should be screaming" in the final scene. [2]
- The song contains a self-critical lyric in which Mayberry contemplates whether she could have avoided the horror if she had "changed my accent, tried to make myself more attractive." [2]
- Described by the Daily Telegraph (2021-08-14) as a "stadium epic" and at the time of publication was unreleased — Screen Violence was due out on 2021-08-27. [2]

Caption: Jack Antonoff "1984 SuperJam" at Bonnaroo 2022 — Lauren Mayberry Guest
Facts:
- Lauren Mayberry performed as a special guest at Jack Antonoff's "1984 SuperJam" — titled "Jack Antonoff 1984" — at Bonnaroo Music Festival on 2022-06-18, held at Great Stage Park. [1,2]
- Mayberry performed two Madonna covers at the SuperJam: "Like a Virgin" and "Material Girl" (songs 2 and 3 of the 15-song set). [1,2]
- Mayberry's SuperJam guest appearance occurred approximately thirty minutes before she and her CHVRCHES bandmates were scheduled to perform their own set on the Which stage at Bonnaroo. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Bonnaroo Music Festival (Which Stage), 18 June 2022
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed on the Which stage at Bonnaroo Music Festival on 2022-06-18. [1]
- Bonnaroo 2022 was CHVRCHES' third appearance at the festival, earning them "Three-Timers Club" status. [2]
- All three members (Lauren Mayberry, Iain Cook, Martin Doherty) were interviewed by The What Podcast at Bonnaroo 2022. [2]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Bonnaroo — Second Appearance (EOE era)
Facts: - Martin Doherty sustained a back injury during CHVRCHES' second Bonnaroo appearance (the visit immediately preceding their 2022 show); the exact year is not given in this source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Shaky Knees Festival, 2022
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Shaky Knees Festival in 2022. [1]
- Trent Reznor of Nine Inch Nails watched CHVRCHES' set at Shaky Knees 2022. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Brooklyn Mirage, New York City, 3 August 2022
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at the Brooklyn Mirage in New York City on 2022-08-03 as part of the Screen Violence touring cycle. [1]
- The set included at least: "Violent Delights," "Final Girl," "Asking for a Friend," "How Not to Drown," "Science/Visions," "Night Sky," "The Mother We Share," "Recover," "Leave a Trace," "Good Girls" (partial setlist). [1]
- "Asking for a Friend" opened the encore. [1]
- For the encore, Mayberry arrived on stage with both arms drenched in blood, subsequently painting parts of her face and torso with it during the performance — an homage to the horror iconography of the Screen Violence era. [1]
- Mayberry executed several quick costume changes during the set, with each look aligned to the ebb and flow of the setlist; her all-black outfit was worn for "Violent Delights" and "Science/Visions." [1]
- Mayberry's custom "Final Girl" T-shirt was worn for the relevant song, described as "a regular now in CHVRCHES sets." [1]
- Consequence described Screen Violence (at the time approaching its one-year anniversary) as CHVRCHES' "excellent fourth album" [studio album], naming the band their "2021 Band of the Year." [1]
- Mayberry has referred to "The Mother We Share," "Recover," and "Leave a Trace" on stage as songs "important to the CHVRCHES lore." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at The Hollywood Bowl, Los Angeles, 25 September 2022
Facts: - CHVRCHES co-headlined The Hollywood Bowl in Los Angeles, CA on 2022-09-25 with Grace Jones, as part of the Screen Violence touring cycle. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at White Oak Music Hall, Houston TX — Screen Violence Tour Opener
Facts:
- White Oak Music Hall, Houston, TX was the first stop of the Screen Violence North American tour; no prior setlist, stage setup, or wardrobe details were publicly known before this show. (Date inferred from the review's 2021-11-14 publication date.) [1]
- Donna Missal opened the show, taking the stage at 6:45 PM. [1]
- The show opened with the Screen Violence static sequence projected on screen; Lauren Mayberry walked out wearing a red trench coat with diamond "blood" tears and makeup, matching the Screen Violence horror aesthetic. [1]
- The set included at least: "He Said She Said" (opener), "Forever," "Leave a Trace," "Violent Delights," "Bury It," "Night Sky," "Never Say Die" (main set closer); encore: "Asking for a Friend," "The Mother We Share," "Clearest Blue" (partial setlist). [1]
- "Violent Delights" was performed live for the first time at this show; the reviewer explicitly noted that "with it being the first show of the tour, they played a lot of new songs for the first time, such as 'Violent Delights.'" [1]
- Before performing "Bury It," Mayberry recalled from the stage a fan video of people singing the "Bury It" synth riff while on Parahoy (Paramore's fan cruise) in 2016. [1]
- A few songs after "Bury It," "Night Sky" (from CHVRCHES' first album) was performed; Mayberry played tambourine during it. [1]
- Mayberry underwent three costume changes during the show: opening in the red trench coat and blood-tear makeup, later changing into a "Final Girl" t-shirt bearing the song's title, and finally appearing covered in fake blood for the encore. [1]
- The main set closed with "Never Say Die," during which Mayberry encouraged the crowd to sing along. [1]
- For the encore, Mayberry returned to the stage covered in fake blood, opening with "Asking for a Friend" before the band performed "The Mother We Share" and closed with "Clearest Blue." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at the Ruby Lounge, Manchester — 25 February 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at the Ruby Lounge, Manchester on 2013-02-25 as part of a UK headline run referred to in the review as "their first headline show, part of a lightning-quick stint across Britain" (understood as the first date of their first proper UK headline tour, not the band's absolute first headline show — the Electrowerkz London show in late 2012 preceded it). [1]
- The show was sold out. [1]
- Support was provided by Conquering Animal Sound. [1]
- The full set comprised 10 songs performed in the following order: "Lies", "We Sink" (referred to in the source as "If We Sink"), "Lungs", "Now Is Not The Time", "Gun", "Science/Visions", "Night Sky", "Recover", "Under The Tide", "The Mother We Share." [1]
- Martin Doherty took lead vocal on "Under The Tide," the penultimate track of the set. [1]
- "The Mother We Share" closed the show. [1]
- Lauren Mayberry delivered stage banter "that was a song about hate" during the set, said after one of the slower mid-tempo tracks in the early portion of the show. [1]
- Mayberry forgot her tambourine at one point during the performance. [1]
- CHVRCHES did not play their "I Would Die 4 U" Prince cover at this show; the reviewer noted its absence while citing it as a known feature of the band's live repertoire, alongside comparisons to The Knife and Prince. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at The Fillmore, Charlotte, NC, 16 October 2018
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at The Fillmore in Charlotte, North Carolina on Tuesday, 2018-10-16 as part of the Love Is Dead tour. [1]
- Support was provided by Lo Moon. [1]
- The complete setlist was: Get Out / Bury It / Gun / We Sink / Graffiti / Graves / God's Plan / Under the Tide / Miracle / Science/Visions / Really Gone / Deliverance / Forever / Recover / Leave a Trace / Clearest Blue; encore: The Mother We Share / Never Say Die. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Targeted by Domain Cybersquatter
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- A US-based scammer registered domain names in CHVRCHES' name and demanded a "substantial sum" from the band to transfer them, plus lifetime memberships of the American Automobile Association (AAA) as part of the extortion. The band never paid. Mayberry stated: "You can't haggle a Glaswegian, it's not going to work." [1]
- CHVRCHES resolved the cybersquatting by registering chvrches.es in Spain, which remained their official website as of 2021-08. Mayberry quoted the band's manager: "Our manager is very smart and said we could get Chvrches.es in Spain and that, to this day, is our website." [1]

Caption: Lauren Mayberry on Table Manners Podcast, August 2021
Facts: - Mayberry appeared on the Table Manners podcast in approximately 2021-08, during Screen Violence promotion, disclosing the specific story behind the "V" spelling in the band's name and the domain cybersquatting incident. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Deftones' Día De Los Festival
Facts:
- CHVRCHES were billed above Gojira at Deftones' Día De Los festival; Hatebreed vocalist Jamey Jasta publicly objected to CHVRCHES receiving a higher booking position than Gojira at the festival. [1]
- When CHVRCHES publicly addressed Jasta's comments, the statement attracted further online abuse directed at the band — one of several incidents in which the band's willingness to speak out was met with increased harassment. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES Statement on Marshmello / Chris Brown Collaboration, and Aftermath
Facts:
- After news broke that Marshmello would collaborate with Chris Brown on a new single — Chris Brown having been found guilty of felony assault following an altercation with Rihanna in 2009 — CHVRCHES issued a public statement saying they were "confused, disappointed and upset" by Marshmello's choice of collaborators. [1]
- The CHVRCHES statement read in part: "Working with people who are predators and abusers enables, excuses and ultimately tacitly endorses that behaviour. That is not something we can or will stand behind." [1]
- Chris Brown responded to the statement by calling the band "a bunch of losers, these are the type of people I wish walked in front of a speeding bus full of mental patients." [1]
- Brown's fans sent CHVRCHES countless death threats following the exchange; Lauren Mayberry was specifically the target of rape threats. [1]
- As a result of the threats, CHVRCHES had to increase their security to finish the US leg of the Love Is Dead tour. [1]
- Mayberry stated in August 2021 that she had "become obsessed with mortality" after 2019: "I was made to think about my demise in a way that was quite jarring, but I also had to continue doing promo and playing shows. People can say 'it's just words on the Internet, it's not reality' but if you read enough things in a day about how people want you to exit the world, it's not so good for your mental well-being." [1]
- Mayberry described the ongoing media coverage of the aftermath — photos of her accompanied by quotes about being terrified of trolls — as itself feeding the misogyny rather than neutralising it. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Edgefest, Frisco, TX, 2016
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at Edgefest in Frisco, TX (North Texas) in 2016; the show was a festival appearance. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at South Side Ballroom, Dallas (Screen Violence Tour)
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed on a Sunday night at South Side Ballroom in Dallas, TX as part of the Screen Violence touring cycle; the show drew a near-capacity audience. [1]
- The show was described as the first non-festival North Texas appearance CHVRCHES had made in six years; prior North Texas appearances cited were a 2019 stop at Fort Worth's Fortress Festival and a 2016 slot at Edgefest in Frisco (both festival bookings). [1]
- The set ran for a little over 85 minutes. [1]
- The band was augmented on stage by touring drummer Jonny Scott. [1]
- The set included at least: "He Said She Said" (opener), "Leave a Trace," "Bury It," and "Final Girl" (partial setlist). [1]
- The stage setup featured a video screen stretching the width of the stage, alternately displaying static macabre images and vivid impressionistic washes of colour, alongside staccato lighting. [1]
- For the encore, Mayberry re-emerged from the wings drenched in fake blood — arms and neck slathered with gore, her white T-shirt stained crimson — and opened with "Asking for a Friend." [1]
- Mayberry quipped between songs: "I know it's a Sunday night, and you could be watching telly, so thanks for coming down." [1]
- The crowd sang back the band's lyrics on multiple occasions during the show with what the reviewer described as "visceral intensity." [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Fortress Festival, Fort Worth, 2019
Facts: - CHVRCHES performed at the Fortress Festival in Fort Worth, TX in 2019; the show was a festival appearance (not a standalone headline show). The Dallas Observer describes it as "Fort Worth's inaugural Fortress Festival" — the accuracy of the word "inaugural" requires corroboration from another source. [1]

Caption: CHVRCHES at Granada Theater, Dallas, TX — 6 June 2013
Era: The Bones of What You Believe
Facts:
- CHVRCHES performed at Granada Theater, Dallas, TX on 2013-06-06; the show was sold out with over 1,000 attendees. [1]
- The set closed with a "sing-along adaptation of Prince's 'I Would Die 4 U'." [1]
- A contemporary reviewer described Lauren Mayberry's stage presence at this show as "throwing minimalist shapes as her thin falsetto rose above the electro din." [1]
